title: History Editing, Rebase, and Selective Transfer · Repository Trace: Cherry Pick Context
summary: Prepare a reviewable local series while preserving authorship intent, conflict evidence, and a recovery reference. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git cherry pick context increment in a history-rewrite plan and verification graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Transfer selected changes while preserving provenance, resolving context, and testing interactions absent from the source branch.
Limit history rewriting using collaboration state, force-with-lease, recovery refs, and explicit coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Cherry Pick Context
steps.instruction:
theory case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cherry Pick Context contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for transfer selected changes while preserving provenance, resolving context, and testing interactions absent from the source branch.

content.text: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Cherry Pick Context condition decided your prediction.

Transfer selected changes while preserving provenance, resolving context, and testing interactions absent from the source branch. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.title: Bound Cherry Pick Context
steps.instruction:
theory case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Cherry Pick Context application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for transfer selected changes while preserving provenance, resolving context, and testing interactions absent from the source branch.

content.text: Transfer selected changes while preserving provenance, resolving context, and testing interactions absent from the source branch. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. A defensible solution applies Cherry Pick Context, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a history-rewrite plan and verification graph.

content.code:
git branch recovery/pre-rebase
git rebase --rebase-merges main
git range-diff recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
git diff recovery/pre-rebase^{tree} HEAD^{tree}
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Cherry-pick copies the original commit object unchanged. This fails the Cherry Pick Context contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Cherry Pick Context evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-cherry-pick-context-1miadi" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Cherry Pick Context into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-cherry-pick-context-1miadi
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]

stimulus.title: Cherry Pick Context failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Transfer selected changes while preserving provenance, resolving context, and testing interactions absent from the source branch.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Cherry Pick Context, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the Cherry Pick Context model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Cherry Pick Context: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Transfer selected changes while preserving provenance, resolving context, and testing interactions absent from the source branch.
